title: In the news #1
content:
h3. That's it! We can't hold it anymore!!

It's official; diesel fuel price is up by 0.60 baht today to 15.19 baht. The government has frozen the price of diesel at 14.59 baht since early January last year. Up to yesterday, the government has paid approximately 3.85 baht per litre to compensate for the price difference. After the price increase, the government still has to pay 2.80 baht diffrence. In total, the government has paid 61,641 baht to compensate for the diesel price (and still paying ...).

What usually happens when diesel price goes up? Prices go up -- every price, everything. --"

However, the government has issued a "NO PRICE INCREASE" warning to everyone in business. The government claims that the increase of diesel price effects only 1% of the overall costs of business. ... No price increase? Reduce the quantity then. Hmmm ...

%(me)ขอภาษไทย
